title: isFuseFs always returns false on Windows
body: filesystems_windows.go has a 'FIXME: implement' comment where isFuseFs() unconditionally returns false, so FUSE-mounted drives on Windows are never categorized correctly in duf's fuseDevice group.

title: --sort has no way to reverse the order
body: main.go exposes --sort mountpoint|size|used|avail|... but there's no --reverse or descending flag, so sorting by usage always lists the smallest filesystems first.

title: --json output omits collected warnings
body: renderJSON() in main.go marshals the []Mount slice directly, but any filesystem read errors collected when --warnings is set aren't included anywhere in the JSON output, unlike the table renderer.

title: Windows Sandbox mount points are only loaded once at startup
body: loadRegisteredWindowsSandboxMountPoints() in filesystems_windows.go reads the registry key once when the package variable is initialized, so isSpecialFs never reflects a sandbox mount point registered after duf starts.
